channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: It's a dangerous complication of pregnancy -- but a new drug holds promise
item.description: Researchers celebrate early results of a drug that may become the first treatment for a serious complication of pregnancy called preeclampsia. It's go...

item.title: American speedskater Jordan Stolz wins second Olympic gold with 500-meter race victory
item.description: With the win, Stolz joins Eric Heiden as the only skaters to take gold in both the 500 and 1,000 at the same Olympics.

item.title: US military reports a series of airstrikes against Islamic State targets in Syria
item.description: The U.S. military says the strikes were carried out in retaliation of the December ambush that killed two U.S. soldiers and one American civilian inte...

item.title: 5 European nations say Alexei Navalny was poisoned and blame the Kremlin
item.description: In a joint statement, the foreign ministries of the U.K., France, Germany, Sweden and the Netherlands say Navalny was poisoned by Russia with a lethal...

item.title: Opinion: Disqualified but not forgotten
item.description: A Ukrainian athlete was disqualified from competition this week by the International Olympic Committee because his helmet had images of other Ukrainia...

item.title: Obama on ICE shootings in Minnesota: "This is not the America we believe in"
item.description: President Obama spoke about the "unprecedented nature" of what he said Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents did in Minnesota.

item.title: Planned US-funded baby vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau blasted by WHO
item.description: Giving some newborns in Guinea-Bissau an established hepatitis B treatment but not others is "unethical", it says.

item.title: Why Gen Z might start turning to matchmakers over dating apps
item.description: Why are more young people choosing to work with matchmakers over dating apps? CBS News associate producer Lauren Fichten speaks to Gen Z New Yorkers a...

item.title: No arrests made after police search residence near Nancy Guthrie's home
item.description: A search has been carried out in Tuscon, Arizona, close to where the 84-year-old was abducted nearly two weeks ago.

item.title: Gen Z is logging off and looking for love this Valentine's Day with matchmakers
item.description: Interest in matchmakers is rising as Gen Z disenchantment with dating apps grows, experts say.

item.title: Russia killed opposition leader Alexei Navalny using dart frog toxin, UK says
item.description: There is no innocent explanation for the toxin being found in samples taken from Navalny's body, Foreign Office says.

item.title: US ocean regulator faces criticism over changes to right whale protection rule
Rubio says US and Europe 'belong together' despite tensions
item.description: The US secretary of state reassures European leaders that the Trump administration backs the transatlantic alliance.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (Feb. 15)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Cuban cigar festival called off as US blockade worsens energy crisis
item.description: The annual aficionados' bonanza has been postponed until further notice as shortages affect international travel.

item.title: Olympic organizers promise Valentine's weekend restock after condoms run out
item.description: Olympic organizers promise that the villages where athletes live won't run out of free condoms again during the Milan Cortina Winter Games.

item.title: TSA agents are working without pay at US airports due to another shutdown
Law enforcement block road near Nancy Guthrieâs home during investigation into her disappearance
item.description: Law enforcement investigating the disappearance of âTodayâ show host Savannah Guthrieâs mother have blocked off a road near Nancy Guthrieâs home in Ar...
